perturbation.original_text: Without the State Bar’s prior written consent, Company will not: (i) disclose to any person or entity the substance or import of the Information; (ii) use any Info rmation to access or obtain additional information outside the purpose contemplated by this NDA; (iii) duplicate, di stribute or otherwise reproduce any Information in any way.
perturbation.changed_text: Company may disclose Information to its affiliates and subcontractors without prior written consent, provided that they are also bound by confidentiality obligations. Company may also use the information for purposes beyond the scope of the RFP.
perturbation.explanation: The original text strictly prohibits disclosure or use of information outside the RFP's scope without written consent. The changed text allows disclosure to affiliates/subcontractors and use beyond the RFP's scope, directly contradicting the original restrictions. This makes the obligations surrounding the provided information uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without the State Bar’s prior written consent, Company will not: (i) disclose to any person or entity the substance or import of the Information; (ii) use any Info rmation to access or obtain additional information outside the purpose contemplated by this NDA; (iii) duplicate, di stribute or otherwise reproduce any Information in any way.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text, and the contradicted text is identical to the original text. The original and contradicted text states that without written consent, the company cannot disclose or use information outside the RFP's scope. However, the changed text now allows the company to disclose information to affiliates/subcontractors and use it for purposes beyond the scope of the RFP, thus creating a direct and strong in-text contradiction according to the structural flaws definition.

perturbation.original_text: Company agrees that it will reta in all Information provided in connection with the RFP in the strictest confidence, and will neither use nor di sclose such Information to anyone other than those who, in each case, need to know the Information for the purpose of responding to this RFP.
perturbation.changed_text: Company agrees to retain all Information, however, the definition of strictest confidence is not applicable and it may disclose such Information to consultants with or without a need to know. The scope of disclosure extends beyond responding to the RFP to allow for internal business purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause insists on the strictest confidentiality. The changed clause eliminates 'strictest confidence' and expands disclosure beyond the RFP's purpose, creating a direct contradiction and uncertainties about confidentiality requirements and the scope of allowed disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company agrees that it will reta in all Information provided in connection with the RFP in the strictest confidence, and will neither use nor di sclose such Information to anyone other than those who, in each case, need to know the Information for the purpose of responding to this RFP. Company will use its best efforts to ensure th at such individuals adhere to the NDA, and Company agrees that it will be responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its employees, agents, authorized representatives or permitted assignees or subcontractors.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text require the 'strictest confidence' in handling information related to the RFP, limiting disclosure. The changed text explicitly states that the definition of 'strictest confidence' is not applicable, and expands the scope of disclosure to consultants, even without a need to know and allows disclosure for internal business purposes beyond responding to the RFP. This directly contradicts the original confidentiality requirements and creates a strong contradiction, as the company is now permitted to disclose information that it was previously obligated to keep confidential.

perturbation.original_text: Company will use its best efforts to ensure th at such individuals adhere to the NDA, and Company agrees that it will be responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its employees, agents, authorized representatives or permitted assignees or subcontractors.
perturbation.changed_text: Company shall not be responsible for any breach of this NDA, irrespective of its employees, agents, authorized representatives or permitted assignees or subcontractors. Company is expected only to inform those said individuals of the NDA.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause holds the company responsible for NDA breaches by its staff and representatives. The changed clause explicitly states the company is *not* responsible, contradicting the previous responsibility clause. The change creates uncertainty about liability for breaches.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company will use its best efforts to ensure th at such individuals adhere to the NDA, and Company agrees that it will be responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its employees, agents, authorized representatives or permitted assignees or subcontractors.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text explicitly states that the company is responsible for any breach of the NDA by its employees, agents, authorized representatives, or permitted assignees or subcontractors. The changed text states that the company shall *not* be responsible for any breach of the NDA, irrespective of those same individuals. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict regarding the company's liability for NDA breaches, aligning with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Upon submission of a proposal to the RFP, or if the State Bar so requests, whichever occurs first, Company will promptly, and at its own expense: (i ) redeliver to the State Bar all copies of the Information in Company’s possession, whether such Information is in written, electronic, digital or other form or format; (ii) destroy any a nd all analyses, compilations, studies, or other documents in any form or format that were prep ared by or for the use of Company which contain or reflect any Information; and (iii) certify such destruction to the State Bar in writing by an authorized officer of the Company who supervised such destruction, as well as the return of all copies as specified above.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon submission of a proposal to the RFP, or if the State Bar so requests, whichever occurs first, Company may retain copies of the Information for archival purposes and destroy analyses at its own discretion. Certification of destruction is not required.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates the prompt return or destruction of all information. The changed clause allows the company to retain copies and gives discretion on destruction of analyses. Also removes the need for certification which all contradicts the previous section creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon submission of a proposal to the RFP, or if the State Bar so requests, whichever occurs first, Company will promptly, and at its own expense: (i ) redeliver to the State Bar all copies of the Information in Company’s possession, whether such Information is in written, electronic, digital or other form or format; (ii) destroy any a nd all analyses, compilations, studies, or other documents in any form or format that were prep ared by or for the use of Company which contain or reflect any Information; and (iii) certify such destruction to the State Bar in writing by an authorized officer of the Company who supervised such destruction, as well as the return of all copies as specified above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the original agreement. Initially, the company was required to return or destroy all information, and certify its destruction. The changed text allows the company to retain copies for archival purposes, destroys analyses at its discretion, and removes the certification requirement. The contradicted text states the company 'will promptly' return/destroy all information and 'certify such destruction'. The changed text allowing retention and discretionary destruction directly contradicts this, as does removing the certification requirement. This creates a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Company's confidentiality oblig ations hereunder will be conti nuing and will survive expiration or termination of the NDA.
perturbation.changed_text: Company's confidentiality obligations hereunder will cease upon expiration or termination of the NDA.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states confidentiality obligations survive the NDA's termination. The changed text states the opposite: confidentiality ends upon termination. This contradiction leads to uncertainty about the longevity of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company's confidentiality oblig ations hereunder will be conti nuing and will survive expiration or termination of the NDA. Company acknowledges and agrees that the reme dy at law for any breach of this NDA is inadequate and that, in additi on to monetary damages, including but not limited to special, incidental, consequential or puniti ve damages, and any other avai lable relief at law, whether based in contract, tort or otherwise, the State Bar will be entitled to specific performance, injunctive relief or any other eq uitable remedy without the need to (i) prove actual damages and/or (ii) post any bond or other security deposit in connection with such remedy.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text state that confidentiality obligations survive the termination of the NDA. The changed text states that these obligations cease upon termination. This directly contradicts the original and contradicted texts, creating a clear and strong in-text contradiction as the clauses are directly opposite in their meaning, creating uncertainty about the longevity of confidentiality, which falls under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
